title: Variable cross section tether
abstract: The exemplary embodiments herein provide a tether for use with an airborne device, where the tether contains an elongate member having a first end for attaching to a ground attachment point and an opposing second end for attaching to the airborne device where the elongate member has a cross-sectional area which varies across the member. In some embodiments, the tether contains one or more electrically conductive elements, an optional strength element, insulation separating any adjacent electrically conductive elements, and a jacket which surrounds and protects each of the tether components.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A tether for use with an airborne device, the tether comprising: an elongate member having a first end for attaching to a ground attachment point and an opposing second end for attaching to the airborne device where the elongate member has a cross-sectional area which varies across the member. 2. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the cross-sectional area of the member varies in discreet variation points. 3. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the cross-sectional area of the member varies continuously across the member. 4. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the cross-sectional area of the member varies near the mid-point of the tether. 5. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the cross-sectional area of the member varies at least 2 times. 6. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the member is comprised of an electrically conductive element wrapped in insulation, a strength member running alongside the electrically conductive element, and a jacket which surrounds the electrically conductive element, insulation, and strength member. 7. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the cross-sectional area of a portion of the elongate member that is near the ground connection point has a larger cross-sectional area than the remainder of the elongate member. 8. The tether of claim 7 further comprising: the cross-sectional area of a portion of the elongate member that is near the attachment with the airborne device has a larger cross-sectional area than the remainder of the elongate member. 9. The tether of claim 1 wherein: the member comprises a first electrically conductive element, a second electrically conductive element which is alongside the first electrically conductive element and separated by a layer of insulation, a strength member alongside the first and second electrically conductive elements, and a jacket which surrounds the first electrically conductive element, second electrically conductive element, insulation, and strength member. 10. A tether for use with an airborne device, the tether comprising: an elongate member having a first end for attaching to a ground attachment point and an opposing second end for attaching to the airborne device where the elongate member is comprised of at least two sections having different cross-sectional areas. 11. The tether of claim 10 wherein: the two sections each comprise approximately half of the total length of the tether. 12. The tether of claim 10 wherein: the elongate member is comprised of a first section which comprises more than half of the total length of the tether and a second section which comprises less than half of the total length of the tether. 13. The tether of claim 10 wherein: the elongate member is comprised of a first section which comprises approximately 60 percent of the total length of the tether and a second section which comprises approximately 40 percent of the total length of the tether. 14. The tether of claim 10 wherein: the cross-sectional area of one of the electrically conductive elements found near a portion of the elongate member that is near the ground connection point has a larger cross-sectional area than the remainder of the electrically conductive element found along the elongate member. 15. A tether for use with an airborne device, the tether comprising: an elongate member comprised of (A) an electrically conductive member, (B) an insulation layer which surrounds (A), (C) a strength member; and (D) an outer jacket which surrounds (A), (B), and (C), where the cross-sectional area of (A)+(B)+(C)+(D) is different at two or more positions along the elongate member. 16. The tether of claim 15 further comprising: (E) a second electrically conductive member inside (D). 17. The tether of claim 16 wherein: (A), (B), (C), and (D) are each co-axial with one another. 18. The tether of claim 15 wherein: 

 the cross-sectional area of (A)+(B)+(C)+(D) at a first position along the elongate member is approximately double the cross-sectional area of (A)+(B)+(C)+(D) at a second position along the elongate member. 19. The tether of claim 18 wherein: approximately 50 percent to 60 percent of the tether has a first cross-sectional area of (A)+(B)+(C)+(D) while the remainder of the tether has a second cross-sectional area of (A)+(B)+(C)+(D), where the second cross-sectional area is smaller than the first cross-sectional area. 20. The tether of claim 19 wherein: the portion of the tether having the second cross-sectional area attaches to the airborne device while the portion of the tether having the first cross-sectional area attaches to a ground connection.
